sample_body_excerpts: Sarah, I am sending this to you to review but a contract to begin working with Peak Sales Recruitment is coming. Tracy wanted a solid firm to do our ISR/AM recruitment that will headhunt for these positions. Our own job postings are not yielding quality candidates and we want to build a pipeline. Jerri Gore, Dina Brouse and Tracy (Bambi and I) met with them today and he has approved our moving forward. Their fee structure is actually less than industry so we will in the long run save money with them instead of the flat 25% fee have paid to Weitz & Associates, and others. I meet with John for my 1:1 today and will also go over with him in more detail. I just wanted to give you the heads up a contract was coming from Carly Van Gogh, BD for Peak. Thank you so very much and please let me know if you have any questions. Donna Millard – Vice President of People & Culture _____________________...

sample_body_excerpts: Tracy, The emails are excessive now. Connor asked us to prepare a letter about TEVET’s dissatisfaction about Peak. You summarize everything we would want to say in that letter below. If we ask for anything at this point, it should be a refund/payback to offset the training costs (below in the chart per hire) for the two replacements we have had to make. The others are working out for now, their model is to replace those that don’t work, so until we know about Preston or Connor, we can leave them out of this equation for now. We also have a decision to make now: 1. Dump Peak and move to Jefferson Wells? My recommendation. 2. If we dump them (Ryan Hofmockel is the last placement with no charge) so let him get hired (Rob liked him and told Emilee to hire). 3. Peak replaces at no charge but we can still argue the training fees with our other dissatisfaction 4. We don’t have a choice about p...

sample_body_excerpts: Haley, Thank you. I reviewed the latest letter. I think it is strong and I do not want to overwork it or delay the response. I do have two important comments before it goes out. First, I want to clarify the mouse-jiggler issue so that the letter is accurate. TEVET did not terminate the ISR specifically for using a mouse jiggler. TEVET’s software identified activity consistent with use of a mouse jiggler. The employee confessed to using it, was written up and counseled, and then later terminated for poor performance. I still think the mouse-jiggler issue is relevant because it is representative of the candidate-quality, judgment, professionalism, and screening concerns TEVET experienced with Peak-sourced candidates. But I do not want Peak to be able to attack the letter by arguing that we misstated the termination reason. I recommend replacing the current mouse-jiggler paragraph with thi...

sample_body_excerpts: Haley, Did a bit more research this weekend. Had CoPilot look through emails and all interactions between our two companies. sample_body_excerpts: CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe. ________________________________ Hi Donna, Thank you so much for this exciting update and for your kind feedback, as well as the opportunity to address fees and work toward a solution. To clarify, if you’re asking about a contingent flat rate (where payment is only due upon placement), that unfortunately isn’t something we’re able to offer at this time. This is due to the significant amount of proactive work, research, and resources that go into our process from the very beginning. That said, if the structure itself isn’t the concern, but rather the overall cost, I’d be happy to explore a solution. Please let me know what per-search amount you feel could bridge the gap, and I’ll begin negotiations on your behalf. Thank you...

sample_body_excerpts: Carly, I presented your information to our sales team today. They are all very excited and wanted to know if they could have 30 minutes of your time to ask you some more questions about Peak’s process and your experience recruiting in our market – Grainger, Neward, Test Equity, and similar companies. Are you available at 9 am EST on September 8? Next Monday? Our CEO is really big on knowing you are finding us relevant candidates in the same market. I explained you had a specific vertical for recruiting in our industry, but he has questions and asked about when we pay the $10k to initiate a search is that per position or per batch of positions, and then $10k for resumes to consider is that for all the negotiated positions we place with you or per position? In any case, the audience would be: Tracy Solomon, CEO Gavin Lessing-Caller, Sales Consultant for TEVET Jerri Gore, Director Sales &...

Ladies and Tracy, I am completing a reference today at 10 am for this company, that frankly Tracy is so aligned to TEVET’s start and mission, they really did their research. I have so many questions for their CEO and will provide you all a summary of my reference once complete. But thought you might like to go to their site directly (link below) to see for yourself. 😊 Donna Millard – Vice President of People & Culture ________________________________________ TEVET - Your VETERAN Source! sample_body_excerpts: Carly, They will have sales questions for you too, so before you engage the account manager, do you want to take this first call? Or would you both be in attendance? For example, today Tracy’s asked me if you all have experience recruiting from Grainger, Newark, NI, Keysight, etc. Those are our competitors. I said you had a vertical in this space but wasn’t sure specifically, could you be prepared to tell them similar clients please. Dina and I are on board with Peak, but Tracy is hard to sell because he want’s the best, which we believe we have found. We just need some help getting over the finish line. Thank you. donna Donna Millard – Vice President of People & Culture ________________________________________ TEVET - Your VETERAN Source! sample_body_excerpts: Carly, I hope you are having a wonderful week. We are getting ready to hire a VP Sales at TEVET. Our current contract is for ISRs and AMs. I think we would need to initiate another contract for this position and we are developing a job description now. Our process for this position will be to bring final candidates in person to interview with the leadership panel. A virtual interview would suffice first. We have struggled to keep a competent leader in this role over the last three years. They would have responsibility for all of our sales team with Jerri Gore, Director Strategic Partnerships and Dina Brouse, Director Sales Operations, reporting to them. The rest of the team totals 23. Do we need to meet again, or can you prepare an agreement that I can get signed to proceed with the executive recruitment team? Thanks. Donna Donna Millard – Vice President of People & Culture ____________...

I understand there are multiple schedules to coordinate and apologize for the inconvenience. Below are days and times I can be available next week: Monday | 9:30-1pm EST Tuesday | 9:30-1:30pm EST Thursday | 9:30-4pm EST Friday | 9:30-4pm EST Best, Beth Terranova On Oct 3, 2025, at 12:51 PM, Donna Millard <donna.millard@tevet.com> wrote: ﻿ ______________________________________________________________________...

sample_body_excerpts: Bojan, I hope you have had a good week so far. At TEVET, when we schedule interviews, we send our Predictive Index (similar to your P95 Assessment) to the interview teams involved in hiring. Sometimes this includes peers of the person interviewing; as was the case for Dina’s ISR positions last week. MY MISTAKE ☹ I did not open the assessments for the three candidates in our ISR pipeline, I just attached them to the meeting invites for each team to review prior to meeting the candidate. I had no idea that desired salary was included in the packets you sent, and it caused some upset among our team, including my CEO today who was very disappointed in me, rightfully so, for not knowing this was included. I have apologized to Dina and our CEO for this. Is there a way moving forward to keep the salary details out of the assessment materials? Please let me know. Thank you! Donna Donna Millard...

Jerri was on this call and TS outwardly stated he didn’t think Dina was working out and that another search may be needed at some point soon. We were reviewing a resume that Jerri made the comment would be a person more suited to Dina’s job and then TS says this. I was a bit disturbed that he would discuss with her peer/and our recruiter so outwardly. 3. Tracy will need to be coached that this time he needs solid documentation if he plans to let her go. sample_body_excerpts: Will want to see them take a swing at more than just one of our target companies before we make any decisions about where we stand on compensation. They have made a cursory sweep and look to have started with the letter “A” on our list of target companies. Hopefully the dive will go much deeper very quickly. JV, cover the commission plan again with Dina. Curious to see Gavin’s feedback on this initial round by Peak. Quick glance. I wouldn’t call these top tier based on experience and track record. Can cover tomorrow. Focus in on your current Newark employees. What were they making at Newark? How long had they be there? They were both several years in position before accepting jobs at TEVET. Happy to leave Newark. Happy at TEVET from what I hear. One is better than the other at our type of work. 1. Janna looks to be out of work. 2. Michael is 30 years in the role. I’m not sure we want so...

sample_body_excerpts: Bojan, We have decided to pursue additional candidates for the inside sales representative. While Bonnie was a good candidate, her salary expectations were too high and we are not going to continue to negotiate with candidates like this. Dina would like to move forward with a new batch of candidates and realizes they may not come with all the experience Janna and Bonnie did. While this is fine, also understand that what we did for Janna, we will not offer to someone with less experience. So target candidates that fit the original financial profile if you will around $65k - $67,500 max. We appreciate you sourcing for us so much. Bonnie just was too focused on money. Thanks! sample_body_excerpts: Ryan / Donna, Thoughts on this version of your Vice President of Sales job posting — rewritten to reflect our latest direction for TEVET, clarify scope (core Test & Measurement business only), reports to me, and have updated the education requirements. Dropped the 10 years of executive experience to 5. Feedback from Peek is that our comp is too low for some experienced candidates. They are getting attention from Sales Directors who want to take the next step. It’s designed to project leadership, mission, and opportunity while inspiring both qualified applicants and referrals. ________________________________ Vice President of Sales — TEVET Delivering Honorable Service to Country, Customer, and Community About TEVET TEVET is a Service-Disabled, Veteran-Owned, and HUBZone-certified small business that serves as a trusted partner to the Federal Government and leading Defense Contractors. H...

He impressed me with his excellent poise and deep knowledge in his current role, which aligns perfectly with the responsibilities of our Inside role. I also appreciate his instinct for selling and that he demonstrates a bit of the hunter mentality in his daily interactions with customers. I would like to move forward with having a second-round interviews. He told me his very interested in moving to the next steps and meeting the team. I will most likely have him speak to the ISR leads and an AM. If all goes well, I will have him meet with Tracy and Jerri as a final. sample_body_excerpts: Bojan, This is great news; thank you! I have cleared Tracy's calendar to ensure that he has 2 full hours with Carla in the office. They will meet from 11am-1pm so they will most likely order in lunch here at the office. We have a wonderful Admin Assistant, Bonnie. Bonnie will work directly with Carla, with me CCed on all emails, to book Carla's flights. Bonnie will also book Carla's hotel and rental car. For incidentals, Carla should keep her receipts and then send them to me for reimbursement. ***Please send the PDF attached to this email to Carla. This Visitors Guide will give Carla a good idea of our local area, the airports, and local hotels. Please let me/Bonnie know if Carla prefers to stay at the General Morgan Inn or the Hampton Inn. Once I receive your reply, I will send an e-introduction email to Carla, Bonnie and you as well. Thank you for your time and I look forward to hear...

sample_body_excerpts: Hi Bojan, (Ben) Jerri forwarded your feedback regarding Ben and his counteroffer. Based on my initial interview with him, increasing the base to $75K is not an option. His current skill set does not align with offering a higher base salary. By comparison, Preston was a stronger candidate and was comfortable with a $72K base. I recommend letting Ben know that we would like to move forward, but that the offer will remain as presented. If he performs well, there will be opportunities for increased compensation as he demonstrates results and growth. If he agrees, we could have something formal when Donna is back on 1/5. (Connor)** Hold off on the offer until Tracy gives me a green light on the comp plan We also just met with Connor. sample_body_excerpts: Hi Boyan- The compensation structure is consistent with what we’ve offered candidates like Janna and Preston, including how we position the variable/uncapped component. I want to ensure Peak feels confident advocating for TEVET’s offering and clearly communicating the value of the ISR role. When candidates ask about compensation, here are a few key points I’d appreciate you reinforcing: * This is a sales role with performance-based commission * Commission is earned and directly tied to results * We’re looking for candidates motivated by growth, performance, and opportunity….not just guaranteed pay * Please emphasize that candidates who lean in, produce results, and take ownership of the role have the opportunity to grow both their career and their commission over time * Key point: TEVET offers a more stable compensation model than many sales organizations due to a higher base-to-commiss...

sample_body_excerpts: Hello Connor, Thank you for your email. I just scheduled Evan Harris with our Advisory Board Member, Sales Leadership Consultant, @Gavin Lessing-Caller <mailto:GLCaller@ZZZConsultingGLC.com> . This Teams meeting/interview is schedule for Monday at 8am PST/9am MST/11am EST. I have added Connor, Stevie, and Jerri as "optional" on the invite as an FYI. Yes, Greg Young is scheduled with Jerri this Monday at 330pm EST. I have sent an invite for this meeting as well. Just to confirm...for Ryan Hofmockel, are you referring to Ryan meeting with Tracy Solomon, TEVET's CEO or with Gavin? There are 2 another email threads for the scheduling with Ryan and Gavin and for the scheduling with Ryan and Tracy. I replied to those emails earlier today. Please review those emails for Gavin and Tracy's availability. I would prefer to keep Ryan's scheduling in those separate email threads. Best regards, Canda...

Connor, Thank you for sending candidates. If you would please in the subject line indicate the position. We are recruiting for both an AM and VP Sales. It makes skimming the emails easier. Look forward to meeting you. Donna Donna Millard – Vice President of HR, People & Culture ________________________________________ TEVET - Your VETERAN Source! Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe. ________________________________ Hi Donna and Jerri, Following up on our conversation last Friday to update you on the ISR replacement search. We met with Dylan Puckett (Connor's referral) and think he would be a strong fit for the role. Please see his attached summary document/resume and let me know if you have any questions. Would you like us to have him complete our Innermetrix assessment before setting up interviews? My team and I have a strong pipeline of candidates that we're engaging. We've had a few other calls so far, and have some promising candidates scheduling for next week. I'll keep you updated and reach out as soon as we have additional candidates ready to present. sample_body_excerpts: Connor, Rob’s interviews have gone really well and we want to bring him to meet with Tracy for a day. Please ask him if he can travel to TN on next Thursday, March 12 in time for a personal dinner with Tracy, and then he can spend all day Friday, March 13 at Headquarters with Tracy. Connor asked me to send him a memo back explaining our position when we last met some time ago. He was to take that message back to his leadership team. They have gotten a ton of money out of us. Emilee got off the phone with Connor today. They have ZERO ISR candidates for us. We are still TWO short after a year of trying. Sarah, HOLD this invoice until we settle up with Peak. You also conveyed real seriousness about the opportunity and about building a long‑term career in Albuquerque, which resonated strongly. Your background and progression made sense, and the way you explained your recent career moves was clear, consistent, and credible.
